title: anthoshorea roxburghii
intro: Anthoshorea roxburghii, also known as Roxburgh's ash or Chiraito, is a plant species that belongs to the family Compositae and is native to the Eastern Himalayas and parts of China. The plant has been traditionally used for its medicinal properties, particularly in treating fever, digestive issues, and skin conditions. The health benefits of Anthoshorea roxburghii include its anti-inflammatory and antiseptic properties, which help in reducing inflammation and promoting wound healing. In herbal preparations, the plant's leaves are often used to make teas, infusions, and decoctions, or dried and used as a powder for topical application.
